subcommands.name: local
subcommands.description: Sets a local application-specific Python version

subcommands.name: global
subcommands.description: Sets the global version of Python to be used in all shells

name: -f
--force
options.description: Install even if the version appears to be installed already

name: -s
--skip-existing
options.description: Skip the installation if the version appears to be installed already

name: -k
--keep
options.description: Keep source tree in $PYENV_BUILD_ROOT after installation

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Verbose mode: print compilation status to stdout

name: -p
--patch
options.description: Apply a patch from stdin before building

name: -f
--force
options.description: Attempt to remove the specified version without prompting for confirmation

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Displays the currently active Python version, along with information on how it was set

subcommands.name: versions
subcommands.description: Lists all Python versions known to pyenv, and shows an asterisk next to the currently active version

name: --bare
options.description: Print only the version names, one per line

subcommands.name: which
subcommands.description: Displays the full path to the executable that pyenv will invoke when you run the given command

subcommands.name: whence
subcommands.description: Lists all Python versions with the given command installed
